<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:يامابوشي]][[ast:Yamabushi]][[co:commons:Category:Yamabushi]][[cs:Jamabuši]][[en:Yamabushi]][[es:Yamabushi]][[fa:یامابوشی]][[fi:Yamabushit]][[fr:Yamabushi]][[it:Yamabushi]][[ja:山伏]][[pl:Yamabushi]][[pt:Yamabushi]][[ru:Ямабуси]][[sk:Jamabuši]][[sv:Yamabushi]][[tr:Yamabushi]][[uk:Ямабусі]][[zh:山伏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:يامابوشي]][[ast:Yamabushi]][[co:commons:Category:Yamabushi]][[cs:Jamabuši]][[en:Yamabushi]][[es:Yamabushi]][[fa:یامابوشی]][[fi:Yamabushit]][[fr:Yamabushi]][[it:Yamabushi]][[ja:山伏]][[pl:Yamabushi]][[pt:Yamabushi]][[ru:Ямабуси]][[sk:Jamabuši]][[sv:Yamabushi]][[tr:Yamabushi]][[uk:Ямабусі]][[zh:山伏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:يامابوشي]][[ast:Yamabushi]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yamabushi]][[cs:Jamabuši]][[en:Yamabushi]][[es:Yamabushi]][[fa:یامابوشی]][[fi:Yamabushit]][[fr:Yamabushi]][[it:Yamabushi]][[ja:山伏]][[pl:Yamabushi]][[pt:Yamabushi]][[ru:Ямабуси]][[sk:Jamabuši]][[sv:Yamabushi]][[tr:Yamabushi]][[uk:Ямабусі]][[zh:山伏]]
{{Short description|Japanese mountain ascetic hermits}} {{Italic title}} {{nihongo3|one who prostrates oneself on the mountain||'''Yamabushi'''}} are Japanese mountain {{ill|Asceticism|en|Asceticism|WD=Q179807|lt=ascetic|1=Asceticism}} {{ill|Hermit|en|Hermit|WD=Q189829|lt=Hermit|1=Hermit}}s.<ref name="Nelson">{{cite book|last1=Nelson|first1=Andrew Nathaniel|title=The Original Modern Reader's Japanese-English Character Dictionary|date=1995|publisher=C. E. Tuttle Co.|location=Rutland, Vermont|isbn=9780804819657|pages=134, 346|edition=Classic}}</ref> They are generally part of the syncretic {{transliteration|ja|[[shugendō]]}} religion, which includes {{ill|Vajrayana|en|Vajrayana|WD=Q172175|lt=Tantric Buddhism|1=Vajrayana}} and [[Shinto]].<ref name="jimon">{{Cite web|title=Exoteric Buddhism, Esoteric Buddhism, and Shugendo - 顕教・密教・修験道|url=http://www.tendai-jimon.jp/trainee/index.html|url-status=live|website=www.tendai-jimon.jp|publisher=Tendaijimon Sect|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201221152905/http://www.tendai-jimon.jp/trainee/index.html|accessdate=31 January 2021|archive-date=2020-12-21}}</ref>
Their origins can be traced back to the solitary [[:en:Yama-bito|Yama-bito]] and some {{nihongo||聖|hijiri}} (saints or holy persons) of the eighth and ninth centuries.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Blacker|first1=Carmen|title=The Catalpa Bow: A Study of Shamanistic Practices in Japan|date=1999|publisher=Japan Library|location=Richmond, Virginia|isbn=1873410859|pages=165–167|edition=3rd}}</ref>
According to American writer {{ill|Frederik L. Schodt|en|Frederik L. Schodt|WD=Q4525911|lt=Frederik L. Schodt|1=Frederik L. Schodt}}:
{{quote|These positively medieval-looking nature worshipers carry metal staves and conch shells and wear straw sandals and sometimes a hemp cloth over-robe with the {{ill|Heart Sutra|en|Heart Sutra|WD=Q622868|lt=Heart Sutra|1=Heart Sutra (interlinear translation)}} written on it. They follow a mixture of esoteric or tantric Buddhism mixed with Shinto, the native {{ill|Animism|en|Animism|WD=Q43004|lt=animistic|1=Animism}} religion of Japan.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Schodt |first=Frederik L. |title=My Heart Sutra |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2020 |isbn=978-1-61172-062-4 |location=Berkeley (Ca)}}</ref>}}
== Clothing and items == [[File:Appearance-of-Yamabushi-rm.jpg|thumb|right|500px|Appearance of the Yamabushi. They wear [[yuigesa]], {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, and {{ill|Tokin (headwear)|en|Tokin (headwear)|WD=Q1580819|lt=tokin|1=Tokin (headwear)}}. They hold a [[:en:shakujō|shakujō]] in their hands, and blow the {{ill|Horagai|en|Horagai|WD=Q655142|lt=Horagai|1=Horagai}} to prevent evil spirits.]] The Yamabushi usually wear and bring the following clothes and items with them:<ref>{{Cite web |last=小項目事典,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,旺文社日本史事典 三訂版,デジタル大辞泉,事典・日本の観光資源,世界大百科事典 第２版,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=山伏(やまぶし)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B1%B1%E4%BC%8F-144308 |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cabello |first=Steve John Powell & Angeles Marin |title=Japan's mountain ascetic hermits |url=https://www.bbc.com/travel/article/20210511-japans-mountain-ascetic-hermits |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=www.bbc.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Citation |title=Yamabushi Ascetic Monk | date=23 January 2017 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6zKJWR-V3GI |access-date=2023-06-02 |language=en}}</ref> * [[Yuigesa]] ({{lang|ja|結袈裟}}), a harness or sash adorned with {{ill|pom-pom|en|pom-pom|WD=Q1187538|lt=pom-pom|1=Pom-pom}}s * {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}} ({{lang|ja|脚絆}}), sandals made from straw * {{ill|Tokin (headwear)|en|Tokin (headwear)|WD=Q1580819|lt=Tokin|1=Tokin (headwear)}} ({{lang|ja|頭巾}}) which is a small hat-like adornment worn at the front of the head * [[:en:shakujō|shakujō]] ({{lang|ja|錫杖}}), a metal rod, held in their hands * {{ill|Backpack|en|Backpack|WD=Q5843|lt=Oi|1=Backpack}} ({{lang|ja|笈}}), backpack * {{ill|Horagai|en|Horagai|WD=Q655142|lt=Horagai|1=Horagai}} ({{lang|ja|法螺貝}}), a conch shell, which they blow like a horn to bind evil spirits
==See also== * {{ill|Cunning folk|en|Cunning folk|WD=Q5194294|lt=Cunning folk|1=Cunning folk}} * [[Mount Ōfuna]] * {{ill|Shaolin Monastery|en|Shaolin Monastery|WD=Q232771|lt=Shaolin Monastery|1=Shaolin Monastery}} * [[:en:Yama-bito|Yama-bito]] * {{ill|Hericium erinaceus|en|Hericium erinaceus|WD=Q19737|lt=Yamabushitake|1=Hericium erinaceus}}
==Further reading== * {{cite book|last1=Ratti|first1=Oscar|last2=Westbrook|first2=Adele|title=Secrets of the Samurai: A Survey of the Martial Arts of Feudal Japan|date=1999|publisher=Castle Books|location=Edison, New Jersey|isbn=0785810730|url-access=registration|url=https://archive.org/details/secretsofsamurai00osca_0}} * {{Cite news | last1 = Powell |first1=Steve John|last2= Cabello| first2 = Angeles Marin | title = Japan's Mountain Ascetic Hermits | newspaper = {{ill|BBC|en|BBC|WD=Q9531|lt=British Broadcasting Corporation|1=BBC}} | date = May 12, 2021 | url = http://www.bbc.com/travel/story/20210511-japans-mountain-ascetic-hermits}}

They wear [[yuigesa]], {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, and {{ill|Tokin (headwear)|en|Tokin (headwear)|WD=Q1580819|lt=tokin|1=Tokin (headwear)}}. They hold a [[:en:shakujō|shakujō]] in their hands, and blow the {{ill|Horagai|en|Horagai|WD=Q655142|lt=Horagai|1=Horagai}} to prevent evil spirits.]] The Yamabushi usually wear and bring the following clothes and items with them:<ref>{{Cite web |last=小項目事典,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,旺文社日本史事典 三訂版,デジタル大辞泉,事典・日本の観光資源,世界大百科事典 第２版,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=山伏(やまぶし)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B1%B1%E4%BC%8F-144308 |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cabello |first=Steve John Powell & Angeles Marin |title=Japan's mountain ascetic hermits |url=https://www.bbc.com/travel/article/20210511-japans-mountain-ascetic-hermits |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=www.bbc.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Citation |title=Yamabushi Ascetic Monk | date=23 January 2017 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6zKJWR-V3GI |access-date=2023-06-02 |language=en}}</ref> * [[Yuigesa]] ({{lang|ja|結袈裟}}), a harness or sash adorned with {{ill|pom-pom|en|pom-pom|WD=Q1187538|lt=pom-pom|1=Pom-pom}}s * {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}} ({{lang|ja|脚絆}}), sandals made from straw * {{ill|Tokin (headwear)|en|Tokin (headwear)|WD=Q1580819|lt=Tokin|1=Tokin (headwear)}} ({{lang|ja|頭巾}}) which is a small hat-like adornment worn at the front of the head * [[:en:shakujō|shakujō]] ({{lang|ja|錫杖}}), a metal rod, held in their hands * {{ill|Backpack|en|Backpack|WD=Q5843|lt=Oi|1=Backpack}} ({{lang|ja|笈}}), backpack * {{ill|Horagai|en|Horagai|WD=Q655142|lt=Horagai|1=Horagai}} ({{lang|ja|法螺貝}}), a conch shell, which they blow like a horn to bind evil spirits
==See also== * {{ill|Cunning folk|en|Cunning folk|WD=Q5194294|lt=Cunning folk|1=Cunning folk}} * [[Mount Ōfuna]] * {{ill|Shaolin Monastery|en|Shaolin Monastery|WD=Q232771|lt=Shaolin Monastery|1=Shaolin Monastery}} * [[:en:Yama-bito|Yama-bito]] * {{ill|Hericium erinaceus|en|Hericium erinaceus|WD=Q19737|lt=Yamabushitake|1=Hericium erinaceus}}
==Further reading== * {{cite book|last1=Ratti|first1=Oscar|last2=Westbrook|first2=Adele|title=Secrets of the Samurai: A Survey of the Martial Arts of Feudal Japan|date=1999|publisher=Castle Books|location=Edison, New Jersey|isbn=0785810730|url-access=registration|url=https://archive.org/details/secretsofsamurai00osca_0}} * {{Cite news | last1 = Powell |first1=Steve John|last2= Cabello| first2 = Angeles Marin | title = Japan's Mountain Ascetic Hermits | newspaper = {{ill|BBC|en|BBC|WD=Q9531|lt=British Broadcasting Corporation|1=BBC}} | date = May 12, 2021 | url = http://www.bbc.com/travel/story/20210511-japans-mountain-ascetic-hermits}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:يامابوشي]][[ast:Yamabushi]][[co:commons:Category:Yamabushi]][[cs:Jamabuši]][[en:Yamabushi]][[es:Yamabushi]][[fa:یامابوشی]][[fi:Yamabushit]][[fr:Yamabushi]][[it:Yamabushi]][[ja:山伏]][[pl:Yamabushi]][[pt:Yamabushi]][[ru:Ямабуси]][[sk:Jamabuši]][[sv:Yamabushi]][[tr:Yamabushi]][[uk:Ямабусі]][[zh:山伏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:يامابوشي]][[ast:Yamabushi]][[co:commons:Category:Yamabushi]][[cs:Jamabuši]][[en:Yamabushi]][[es:Yamabushi]][[fa:یامابوشی]][[fi:Yamabushit]][[fr:Yamabushi]][[it:Yamabushi]][[ja:山伏]][[pl:Yamabushi]][[pt:Yamabushi]][[ru:Ямабуси]][[sk:Jamabuši]][[sv:Yamabushi]][[tr:Yamabushi]][[uk:Ямабусі]][[zh:山伏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:يامابوشي]][[ast:Yamabushi]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yamabushi]][[cs:Jamabuši]][[en:Yamabushi]][[es:Yamabushi]][[fa:یامابوشی]][[fi:Yamabushit]][[fr:Yamabushi]][[it:Yamabushi]][[ja:山伏]][[pl:Yamabushi]][[pt:Yamabushi]][[ru:Ямабуси]][[sk:Jamabuši]][[sv:Yamabushi]][[tr:Yamabushi]][[uk:Ямабусі]][[zh:山伏]]
{{Short description|Japanese mountain ascetic hermits}} {{Italic title}} {{nihongo3|one who prostrates oneself on the mountain||'''Yamabushi'''}} are Japanese mountain {{ill|Asceticism|en|Asceticism|WD=Q179807|lt=ascetic|1=Asceticism}} {{ill|Hermit|en|Hermit|WD=Q189829|lt=Hermit|1=Hermit}}s.<ref name="Nelson">{{cite book|last1=Nelson|first1=Andrew Nathaniel|title=The Original Modern Reader's Japanese-English Character Dictionary|date=1995|publisher=C. E. Tuttle Co.|location=Rutland, Vermont|isbn=9780804819657|pages=134, 346|edition=Classic}}</ref> They are generally part of the syncretic {{transliteration|ja|[[shugendō]]}} religion, which includes {{ill|Vajrayana|en|Vajrayana|WD=Q172175|lt=Tantric Buddhism|1=Vajrayana}} and [[Shinto]].<ref name="jimon">{{Cite web|title=Exoteric Buddhism, Esoteric Buddhism, and Shugendo - 顕教・密教・修験道|url=http://www.tendai-jimon.jp/trainee/index.html|url-status=live|website=www.tendai-jimon.jp|publisher=Tendaijimon Sect|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201221152905/http://www.tendai-jimon.jp/trainee/index.html|accessdate=31 January 2021|archive-date=2020-12-21}}</ref>
Their origins can be traced back to the solitary [[:en:Yama-bito|Yama-bito]] and some {{nihongo||聖|hijiri}} (saints or holy persons) of the eighth and ninth centuries.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Blacker|first1=Carmen|title=The Catalpa Bow: A Study of Shamanistic Practices in Japan|date=1999|publisher=Japan Library|location=Richmond, Virginia|isbn=1873410859|pages=165–167|edition=3rd}}</ref>
According to American writer {{ill|Frederik L. Schodt|en|Frederik L. Schodt|WD=Q4525911|lt=Frederik L. Schodt|1=Frederik L. Schodt}}:
{{quote|These positively medieval-looking nature worshipers carry metal staves and conch shells and wear straw sandals and sometimes a hemp cloth over-robe with the {{ill|Heart Sutra|en|Heart Sutra|WD=Q622868|lt=Heart Sutra|1=Heart Sutra (interlinear translation)}} written on it. They follow a mixture of esoteric or tantric Buddhism mixed with Shinto, the native {{ill|Animism|en|Animism|WD=Q43004|lt=animistic|1=Animism}} religion of Japan.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Schodt |first=Frederik L. |title=My Heart Sutra |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2020 |isbn=978-1-61172-062-4 |location=Berkeley (Ca)}}</ref>}}
== Clothing and items == [[File:Appearance-of-Yamabushi-rm.jpg|thumb|right|500px|Appearance of the Yamabushi. They wear [[yuigesa]], {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}}, and {{ill|Tokin (headwear)|en|Tokin (headwear)|WD=Q1580819|lt=tokin|1=Tokin (headwear)}}. They hold a [[:en:shakujō|shakujō]] in their hands, and blow the {{ill|Horagai|en|Horagai|WD=Q655142|lt=Horagai|1=Horagai}} to prevent evil spirits.]] The Yamabushi usually wear and bring the following clothes and items with them:<ref>{{Cite web |last=小項目事典,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,旺文社日本史事典 三訂版,デジタル大辞泉,事典・日本の観光資源,世界大百科事典 第２版,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 |title=山伏(やまぶし)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B1%B1%E4%BC%8F-144308 |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cabello |first=Steve John Powell & Angeles Marin |title=Japan's mountain ascetic hermits |url=https://www.bbc.com/travel/article/20210511-japans-mountain-ascetic-hermits |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=www.bbc.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Citation |title=Yamabushi Ascetic Monk | date=23 January 2017 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6zKJWR-V3GI |access-date=2023-06-02 |language=en}}</ref> * [[Yuigesa]] ({{lang|ja|結袈裟}}), a harness or sash adorned with {{ill|pom-pom|en|pom-pom|WD=Q1187538|lt=pom-pom|1=Pom-pom}}s * {{ill|Kyahan|en|Kyahan|WD=Q6403679|lt=Kyahan|1=Kyahan}} ({{lang|ja|脚絆}}), sandals made from straw * {{ill|Tokin (headwear)|en|Tokin (headwear)|WD=Q1580819|lt=Tokin|1=Tokin (headwear)}} ({{lang|ja|頭巾}}) which is a small hat-like adornment worn at the front of the head * [[:en:shakujō|shakujō]] ({{lang|ja|錫杖}}), a metal rod, held in their hands * {{ill|Backpack|en|Backpack|WD=Q5843|lt=Oi|1=Backpack}} ({{lang|ja|笈}}), backpack * {{ill|Horagai|en|Horagai|WD=Q655142|lt=Horagai|1=Horagai}} ({{lang|ja|法螺貝}}), a conch shell, which they blow like a horn to bind evil spirits
==See also== * {{ill|Cunning folk|en|Cunning folk|WD=Q5194294|lt=Cunning folk|1=Cunning folk}} * [[Mount Ōfuna]] * {{ill|Shaolin Monastery|en|Shaolin Monastery|WD=Q232771|lt=Shaolin Monastery|1=Shaolin Monastery}} * [[:en:Yama-bito|Yama-bito]] * {{ill|Hericium erinaceus|en|Hericium erinaceus|WD=Q19737|lt=Yamabushitake|1=Hericium erinaceus}}
